milestoneTitle: Phase M9.19a.3 — Grab icon prominence + dashboard parity. The ⋮⋮ glyph inside .dash-tile-grab bumped 14x18 -> 18x22 via a new .dash-tile-grab svg CSS rule so it reads clearly at arm's length on phone; the 28x44 button dimensions stay the same, keeping the leading-column geometry locked. renderDashLearn now emits data-sortable='1' on real lesson tiles (placeholder dummy stays non-sortable — it's a CTA, not real content), and wireLearnGrabHandle is wired in the post-render loop so dashboard Learn tiles get the same drag-trace feedback as the Learn list. Drop semantics on dashboard aren't wired yet — no .learn-zone-upnext / .learn-zone-all containers exist on the dashboard surface, so releases ease the tile home via resetRowTransform — real dashboard-sort behavior lands in a follow-up once we decide whether drag should pin/unpin, swap positions, or promote from elsewhere. Projects dashboard tiles still keep their grab reserved-invisible (no well-defined sort semantics there yet either). Renders as user expected: consistent grab area across dashboard + Learn list, icon more noticeable, blank-space aesthetic preserved.
